title: Tasmil Finance
tagline: An autonomous DeFi yield agent that hires ACP specialists for intelligence and executes on-chain itself — never handing over custody.
description: Tasmil is an autonomous DeFi yield optimizer. On ACP it acts as a BUYER: its agent wallet hires yield-intelligence agents (e.g. Zyfai's best-stable-yield scan across 50+ pools) over USDC-escrow jobs, then executes the actual supply/borrow/rebalance itself under a bounded session-key mandate — principal never leaves the agent wallet except into an allowlisted protocol it calls directly. Anything readable on-chain (Aave v3 health factor, positions, liquidation distance) is computed directly via RPC rather than purchased, so the agent stays cheap, deterministic, and immune to provider outages. Ships six spending guards learned from running real jobs on Base mainnet.

skills.name: tasmil-defi-agent
skills.summary: Hire ACP specialist agents as a buyer with hard spending caps: discover an online provider, place a capped job, fund on budget.set, retrieve the deliverable, complete or reject+refund on Base. Ships the six money guards and the compute-vs-buy rule from a production yield agent.

visual.title: autonomous yield agent that hires, then executes itself

artifacts.label: Real ACP job 70243 (Tasmil hired Zyfai best_stable_yield on Base) + Aave risk brain
Compute-vs-buy: self-computed Aave v3 risk reader (aave-hf.mjs)
Promo video (Tasmil ACP buyer + on-chain risk brain)
Tool-card gallery — Aave v3 reads + signed supply/borrow/swap/zap on Base
Agent wallet dashboard — portfolio, activity & tokens
Chat: best stable yield — ACP scan unavailable, falls back to on-chain Aave read
Chat: agent reads on-chain wallet balance on Base
Chat: on-chain Aave v3 health-factor check (1.10, at-risk)
Chat: one-signature zap — USDC to GHO supplied on Aave v3
MetaMask signing — user approves the zap in-wallet (custody stays with user)
Chat: post-supply position — GHO supplied, no debt, no liquidation risk
In-app swap — token selector on Base

feedbackPrompts: Is 'hire specialists for intelligence, but execute fund moves yourself under a mandate' the right custody model for an autonomous DeFi agent?
Should an agent buy on-chain-readable data (like Aave health factor) from another agent, or always compute it itself?
Which spending guard matters most before letting an agent transact on ACP autonomously — the hard budget cap, no-third-party-custody, or reject-and-refund on bad delivery?
